title: Production K8s pods crashing — energy reporting service
symptoms: Multiple pods entering CrashLoopBackOff state. Energy reporting dashboard inaccessible for 22 minutes.
root_cause: Resource limits set too low (128Mi memory limit). Application memory usage spiked during peak data ingestion window.
resolution: Increased memory limit to 512Mi and request to 256Mi. Added HPA to auto-scale during peak windows. Deployed fix in 28 minutes.

title: Jenkins pipeline broken — artifact push to Nexus failing
symptoms: All builds succeeding through test stage but failing at artifact publish. Deployment pipeline blocked for 3 hours.
root_cause: Nexus repository credentials rotated but not updated in Jenkins credential store. Certificate also expired on Nexus server.
resolution: Updated Jenkins credentials with new Nexus service account password. Renewed Nexus TLS certificate. Re-ran affected pipelines.

title: AWS EC2 CPU spike — fossil data aggregation service
symptoms: CPU utilization hit 98% on m5.xlarge instance running data aggregation service. Response times degraded to 12 seconds.
root_cause: Unoptimized SQL query introduced in recent release performing full table scan on 40M row dataset. No index on timestamp column.
resolution: Added composite index on timestamp + plant_id columns. Query time reduced from 8.2s to 0.04s. CPU normalized within 5 minutes of index creation.

title: Terraform apply failed — VPC module state lock
symptoms: Terraform apply blocked with state lock error. New environment provisioning stopped. DevOps team blocked for 45 minutes.
root_cause: Previous terraform apply was interrupted mid-run due to network timeout. State lock was not released automatically.
resolution: Confirmed no active Terraform runs. Used terraform force-unlock with the lock ID from the error message. Resumed apply successfully.

title: Prometheus alert — generation monitoring service down
symptoms: Prometheus fired ProdServiceDown alert at 03:14 UTC. Generation monitoring dashboard showed no data. Alert resolved at 03:31 UTC.
root_cause: OOM kill on the monitoring service pod. Memory limit was 256Mi but spike to 310Mi occurred during large data batch processing.
resolution: Increased memory limit to 512Mi. Added memory-based HPA. Added pre-emptive alert at 80% memory utilization to catch before OOM.
